sentences.text: Good morning!
Hey there!
Hello, how are you?
Good, how are you?
Doing well.
Reese should be joining, I think she confirmed.
Yep, she's happening right now.
Good.
How have you been?
How was your weekend?
Guess it's Tuesday now, but…
It was good, it was busy.
Went by a lot quicker than I had hoped for, but that's okay.
There's another one coming up, so…
How was yours?
Mine was good!
I had a weekend at home, which was really nice.
So… I feel like I've been doing something every weekend for most of the summer, so it was really nice to be in Madison and do some Madison things.
We hit up the farmer's market.
Oh, nice!
Project's done… it was good.
Oh, yeah.
It's nice to have low-key weekends like that every once in a while, for sure.
Hello, Reece!
Hello, how are you guys?
Good, how are you?
I'm good!
So sorry I was a little bit late.
My Zoom likes to always do whatever it likes to do when I'm getting on Zoom meetings, and I'm just like, well, I don't know what you're downloading, but that's okay with me.
Zoom is always downloading, we're always downloading something.
downloading something, I'm like, whatever, that's good.
If it'll let me get on.
It's the same for me, but with Teams.
So we use Zoom, so it's anytime a customer drops me a Teams link, it's always, like, a 5-minute ordeal to get in, so…
We're the opposite.
We usually use Teams, so Teams is super easy, but then sometimes Zoom, my computer's like, wait, what?
What are we doing over here?
Well, we can always use your link next time if it's easier.
while to figure out how to share my screen.
That is a-okay.
Reese, where are you located?
I am in Austin, Texas.
Nice.
Yes, where are you located?
All three of us are in Wisconsin.
Oh, amazing, amazing.

I'm actually doing some… I haven't been, no, but I've been doing some family lineage recently, and my… grandmother's parents, their parents are from Wisconsin, and I did not know that, and so now I'm like, well, I gotta figure out what's in the Wisconsin, because some of my family's from there.
This came up on a call yesterday in my.
Sure did.
Oh, really?
There is a lot of cheese here.
There's a lot more than cheese.
A lot of lakes.
The Packers.
Packers?
Packers?
Where are your grandparents, Reece?
Where are they?
My grandparents… so, all of my family, I'm the only oddball out of my family, they all live in Fort Worth, Texas, and I'm the only one that lives, away from everyone, so it's kind of funny.
It's kind of a weird thing, you know?
It's like, they view me as, like, this big city girl, and I'm just in Austin, Texas, you know?
And…
Sorry, did you say your grandparents are from Wisconsin, or somebody else?
My… my grandparent… so my…
great-grandparents.
Oh, okay.
Great, great.
I'm just gonna start asking people where their grandparents are from, I love that.
Okay, so they're from Fort Worth.
That's a good, that's a good little icebreaker.
As soon as you said Fort Worth, I realized I probably didn't catch who was from Wisconsin correctly on the front end.
My great…
great parent… great parents, so… a few generations back.
I don't know why they ended up moving to Texas.
Because my grandmother, she was born in Texas, so you need to figure out why they made that move from up north to down south.
Probably winter.
Probably so.
Oh, I think over… maybe you told me if I'm wrong, Reese, but a lot of people are looking at that heat in Texas, and they're starting to think, I don't know, maybe the winters sound a little nice.
Absolutely.

Living in Austin, there are a lot of people who, you know, have never lived in Texas before, and just now moving, and they don't understand that, like, right now it's 90 degrees, it's not getting to, like…
Yeah.
70 degrees during the days until late October, early November.
And when I say that, people are like, no, that's not true.
I'm like, yes, it is.
We will not be wearing sweaters until January.
Like, it just does not work like that.
So, one day I'll live in a… a state where I can have some nice winter, winter fashion.
Well, I would say, if you're going to come visit, you should come in the summer.
I bet it's beautiful, all the green.
It is.
We have every season here, but for the first time, I would definitely recommend the summer.
Not… don't come in the winter.
Amazing.
Well, Reece, how long have you been with Asset?
In a few days, it'll be 3 years, so…
Wow.
I've been here for a little bit, a little bit, yes ma'am.
Nice.
And tell me a little bit about your role.
So I… I'm on Jacqueline's team, we are the marketing support team.
So… I bet Jacqueline kind of explained what our whole team does, just kind of helping the properties who do not have designated marketing help by a particular marketing manager.
But then my kind of niche in that is online reputation.
So, I am majority doing kind of the onboarding and the auditing, with Jacqueline's help, of course.
you know, anything that the properties need from us to be successful on those platforms, we will do.
I will do.

So, yes, ma'am, kind of everything on the back end, And just making sure that all the properties are kind of doing what they should be doing, they're attending trainings, they're logging in, I don't know what that would be, maybe, like, housekeeping stuff.
So, yeah, that's kind of the majority of what I do.

Yeah, and just to piggyback off of, Reese, one of the main reasons that I wanted her to get an introduction to y'all and to the platform itself, I did speak with Saad last week, and we do want to start pushing Maven, so I'll get with you, sidebar.
you know, in regards to… to kind of what he and I talked about, and I think I may need, like, a one-pager from you guys if you have that, but we can discuss that later on.
But I really wanted her to get kind of acclimated.
Saad does want us to take on any onboarding and work hand-in-hand with you guys going forward.
So, obviously, myself and Reece take care of, like, the reputation, social, and listings piece.
from other providers, so it just made sense to kind of get her, you know, filled in on what you guys do, and kind of what the platform does, and the implementation, and whatnot.
So, kind of wanted just to run through that today with her, see if she had any questions, and go from there.
Awesome.
Cool, yeah.
We… the back, story at Reese is that we have been working with the asset team for the last couple years on our Google Business automation tool.
So we've recently been talking with Saad and team about a larger rollout for our Google tool and our social automation tools.
So we have been talking about ease of implementation and how to get these properties up and running a little quicker.
Are you familiar with Smartsheet?
I am not.
No, ma'am.
Okay.
Yep, so we have a… let me share my screen and show you what it looks like.
So we have a shared spreadsheet, called Smartsheet that we've used for other companies similar to Asset to get onboarding, kind of sped up.
So, in this Smartsheet, it's a form where as soon as you enter something into it, our team gets pinged.
So, our implementation team's also in on this.
So, as soon as a property's added, they will get the notification, and then they can start setup right away.
Oh, that's nice, okay.
So, the things entered in here would be property name, address, who input the property.
A big piece of this, so we take over the automation and optimization of Google profiles.
And what we need for setup is the property management software feed turned on, so the syndication turned on, just like you would for an ILS.
And Reese, just to interject here, they're already working with Brandon, so that's… that box is already checked.
Amazing, okay.
Christian's already about that.
Yep.
So, perfect.
Yeah.
Yeah, we have about 100 properties live with Asset right now, and about 60 of those were recently added that Brandon's doing implementation for right now.
Okay.
Yep.
So, the other piece is Google Access turned on, so we give your team a group code, in lieu of, like, an email address.
Brandon's handling all of that.
So, that's what these boxes would be.
And then we have a social automation tool as well that your team has started working with us on.
So that's, automated social posts to Instagram and Facebook, that's what these checkboxes are.
Entering a UTM code for tracking purposes.
And then the property email.
So, that's all that's needed for sign-up, and then our team takes it over from there.
Who do you guys usually work with for the UTM code?
Is that someone from… Maybe poetic, from, like, the website side, because they would have the analytics.
Morgan, do you know who's sending us those?
I don't know who is sending them, I just know they're coming from… from your end, yeah.
You guys are getting them, okay.
We're getting them from somewhere, yeah.
That works.
These are blank because this is new for us, working with assets.
So, up until now, we've just been adding property by property.
We have been doing, like, one-off contracts.
So, that's why none of this is filled in right now, but, all new properties after this will have these filled out.
Okay.
Kylie, can you just remind me, too, what the implementation timeline is like?
Is it… once you guys get the information plugged in on the Smartsheet from our side, is it roughly 3 to 5 days?
Is it longer?
Like, what can we expect?
Yep.
I think we're about 4 to 5 business days right now, and that is from the time the property's entered into the Smartsheet.
The only caveats would be if there's a delay in us getting the feed, that can sometimes… Obviously, the property has to be verified.
Sometimes we'll get properties added that the page isn't verified.
But yeah, on average, it's about 3-5 business days.
Okay.
Any questions that you guys have on this process?
Mmm, bye.
You're good, Reece?
At this moment, I don't.
I feel, though, once I start getting into it, I might.
Maybe a little bit logistics things, like… Let's say, for example… Brandon's team is having a little bit of a hard time with integration.

if me or our team were to give you all the information you need, kind of on this sheet, would you be able to take anything live at all, or would you need Brandon to complete his part before you took anything live?
We would need Brandon to complete his part, because we need… these are the two key pieces, the feed and the Google access.
So how the product works is… we… I can actually show you just, like, a one sheet here.
How the product works is we take the information from the feed, and we use our internal, automation and AI tool to create the content on the profile.
So we're taking in pricing and availability, photos, amenity-based information, all from the feed.
So that's, like, the source of truth for all of the information.
And then, in order for us to actually post it, we need that access piece.
Okay.
So…
Did that answer your question?
Yes, it does.
I can definitely… I can easily give you access, like, Google Business access, but I can't do the integrations.

Yeah, the PMS integrations will come from the systems… the system side, so they… they'll continue to work with Brandon on that piece, and then, I would imagine he'd probably pass off the Google My Business integration piece to us.
It's totally fine, I can definitely…
Yep.
Brandon's been super fast, too, so I think we've seen that work, where Brandon turns on… the feed, and then… it's the same code every time, Reese, you just input it into the profile.
I think we could probably share…
Yeah.
what that looks like.
Reese, Jacqueline mentioned that she's used to Smartsheet with Opinion, I think you mentioned, Jacqueline.
Is that… Are you part of the opinion process, too, or not?
It's actually a Google Doc with Opinion.
We use Smartsheet with our HR department.
Okay.
Yeah, apologies on that, it totally…
Okay.
Yep.
I think, like, this is basically a Google Sheet, Reese, it's just anytime that you all fill out a, sell, like, to add a property, it automatically triggers our implementation team.
So…
That makes sense.
It's, it's like a… basically the same thing as Google Docs.
more depth.
We don't touch… like anything… On the left side of the… of the, check marks that are on there right now.
Like, where I'm not adding names.
God.
You guys are adding that, and then from there, we're adding our information, is that correct?
No.
So…
You plug it in, the property information, the name, I assume the group code, too, is that correct?
And then, community address.
Yeah, so everything that's blue would be on assets side.
So the group code, it's always the same, so we input those, and I'll just keep dragging that down, it's the same Is that property?

But then it would be… the name and address would be of the properties that you're requesting this to be added for, so I don't know if that would be your team, or if we would have others involved that would be inputting names.
Yeah.

Ideally, it's going to run through a ticketing system, so if it is a marketing manager that oversees a property, say, like, Shelby, or Monica, or whomever, they'd be putting in a ticket, come to the centralized team, which is our team, and we can actually have, like, some automation set up to where they're already providing this information for us, so it's simply us just going in and plugging in.
So it's less hands on this sheet, which I think is probably Better, but that's… that would more than likely be our process going forward.
Yep, that works for us.
Okay.
That makes sense.
That makes sense.
Okay, cool.
Because I… can we only… can y'all only have this given access to me and Jacqueline?

Like, can no one else have access to this on the… So I… just because sometimes, like, with… what I like about We Work With Opinion is that not everyone has access to that sheet, and so there's not a lot of, you know, like, cookie… like, hands in the cookie jar type thing, and so… it won't get messed up, and so that's the only thing that I, you know, get worried about, is, like, if me and Jacqueline have a system that we do, and then someone else has access to it, then maybe that can… mess things up a little bit, so if me and Jacqueline can only have access to this, or anyone else Jacqueline wants, like, saw it or something like that, that would be great.
Yep.
Yeah, I would think probably Brandon would maybe need access as well, if he's doing the, the feed part.
Yes.
So yeah, yeah, we can have whoever you want set up on it.
So if it's just the three of you for now, and then once we get the process down, if we open it up to other people, we can do that.
They remind me, too, how does it trigger for the contract signature?
Since it's an MSA, are you guys just adding it to the MSA?
It's not individual contracts that have to be signed?
That's right, yeah.
So we are still working with David on that, but that's how we have it for another customer that's doing this.
We signed the one MSA, and then any property that's signed up… say we signed a… you added a property today, that property would start implementation now, and then the billing start would be 10-1.
So everything within that month would just get started, with the next billing cycle.
Gotcha, great.
That's good to know.
Eliminates the need to send contracts back and forth, because we have.
And say.
Absolutely.
No, that's very helpful.

We have it… unfortunately, we have it to where Opinion is sending us contracts after we, fill out their Google Docs, so it's kind of… and that's another reason, I think, why Reece is saying if we could limit how many people have access to this, because, you know, otherwise people start going in and plugging stuff in.
I'm not sure, you know, that they've done that, and then contracts are… it just gets really hairy, so… we can keep it limited, I think that's probably, more ideal.
Yeah, I think so as well.
H.
The things that we're entering on our end, so you're aware, we're… our implementation team is going in and checking when we receive Google Access, if there is an unverified page.
Sometimes pages get unverified after they're added, so we'll flag that in here if we see any get unverified.
When we receive the feed, and then our team will input the date that it went live.
Perfect.
Yep.

In regards to, well, I guess you guys don't need to do, like, trainings with on-site teams, or have you guys done anything like that with the properties that are already on, just so they're aware of the product and kind of what it's doing for them?
We… we really haven't.
We haven't gotten the request to.
Okay.
So, the workflow we've had up until now has been we've been working with Shelby and her team, Rosalie and some of her team.
They'll just add the properties, and then our ongoing communication is with those teams.
Perfect.
Yeah, I think that makes… I think that makes sense, too, and Risa, you know, this tool is basically, like, hands-off for the on-site teams.
Because of the integrations piece, like, they really shouldn't be having to pop in there and update much.
We did talk, and maybe, Kylie can kind of go over this, they have some other products.

I know that Saad really wanted to focus on the Google AI piece and getting that It's implemented across the board, but they do have, like, a social piece, and they do have the ability to go in there and change, but really.
It should be totally hands-off for the on-site teams, and that's… that's really the push for this product to, you know, a big benefit of it.
This is in… this is incredible.
I love… I love this.
Love to hear that.
Yeah, we have been talking, about our social tool as well.
So, we went over the Google tool a little bit, very high level, but, and it's really to automate processes that teams are doing.
So, whether that's on-site teams that are manually posting to your Google profile or your social pages.
Or if you're using other tools, to automate it, it's meant to fill out more areas throughout the profile.
I think the biggest piece that, for the Google tool, that we had interest in from Asset was our pricing and availability feed.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
And that pulls directly from website, correct?
Directly from the property.
Yes.
Oh, the PMS, okay, even better, even better.
Yeah, so this is pulling live pricing and availability at the floor plan level, updated every day.
Amazing.
Could you send this, deck to me by chance?
Just so I can look over it and whatnot?
I have it, I can flip it.
Yeah, okay, cool.
Perfect.
I love this.
Super, super easy.
Like, And I love that y'all don't have to… and I don't mean this in a bad way, but interact with the on-site team at all.
You know, it's all just, like, on the back end of stuff.
It just makes it… makes it a lot easier.
Yeah.

Yeah, it's really just pulling that information from the feed to show what's available in the property right now for pricing and availability, and then we highlight amenities, so it's all… for the Google side, it's all property-specific content, showing what amenities the property has.
So it's pretty straightforward, information.
And then the social side, similar concept to Google, where it's syndicated with the feed, it's, posting automatically using our AI tool.
The social tool is posting on Instagram and Facebook, and then the content's different than what we're posting on Google.
So we incorporate some, like, lifestyle, holidays, tour scheduling, so the social tool is different content than what's going to Google.
Okay.
Nice.
I love.
And you… do you have a…
What am I trying to say?
Silly question, but where do you get the photos to post, or where do you get all of, you know, this information?
Also from the PMS, also from the Google?
Yep, for the social, so for all of it, we're getting all of this information from the feed.
So that's the photos, the amenity-based information, pricing availability, all from the feed.
For the social tool that you're looking at right now, we do layer on top some stock photography to highlight tour scheduling, pet policy.
Typically, you don't have photos for a pet policy, so we'll pull some stock photos for that and for holidays.
But everything else is right from the feed.
Property-specific, too.
Okay.

if the property is interested, I don't know if y'all have this option, but if they wanted more of their, like, let's say they were against stock photos, and they didn't want stock photos, is there a way for us to send you photos from a certain property to post?
I know this is a kind of a far-fetched question, but it just came to mind.
Not far-fetched, we can definitely do that.
Okay.
The…
We'll take everything from the feed, but then we can also layer on top, photography.
If you have, like, a Dropbox, link of other photos that you want, we can layer that on top.
Okay, cool.
Perfect, because we do have some… like, higher class communities that I might, like, are super, super good with their social media, so they might be interested in, like, more of their photos.
incorporated with this, so… amazing.
Amazing.
I have a quick question.

I failed to ask last time we met, and this is going back to the Google AI tool, but… Do you guys ever, like, flag or notify us if someone were to mark the property as, like, permanently closed, or, like, the hours being inaccurate and trying to change those?
Do you guys ever handle, like, that piece at all?
I know it's rare, it can be rare, but we have seen that happen.
So you're saying if a property closed, like, the Google page was inactive and the property was closed, if we would stop… if we would get notified to notify you?
Like, if someone were to mark it as permanently closed.
And maybe that's something people can't do anymore.

I know a couple years ago, we had issues where, like, maybe it was, like, a past resident that we had issues with, and we're just going in there to mark the property as being closed, and I assume it was after so many people did that, then Google flagged it as permanently closed, and then we had to go through a whole process to, like.
re-verify it.
Do you guys, like, flag us for anything like that that may come up?
Yeah, we… we have, a report right now that will let us know if anything happens to the profile that would prevent us from posting, so.
Okay.
We lose access, if we need you to, like, re-verify the property.
We… basically, we will get notified if we can't post anything, and then we'll usually get notified for the exact reason why, and then what you would need to do to fix it.
So, yes, we have that report in place, and that's something I look at about weekly, and would just reach out, to you guys and let you know.
That's true.
Right.

Like, hours, as far as, like, the hours and, like, the listings management side of that, we, like, we don't, we wouldn't get any notifications for incorrect hours or anything that we're not managing the pieces of.
Okay, so if they were to enroll with the listings piece of the product, that would be included.
Yeah, that… we have… it's like a separate product that we sell, and you would have your own dashboard, that would allow you to go in and update, like, business hours, address, those directory.
Yes.
Foundational pieces, and then that would… the dashboard would notify you if anything were to be incorrect.
Okay.
And we have a thing for that in front of David and Saad.
It's part of the MSA, where you lock in pricing.
I'm gonna forget what it is off the top of my head.
I think it was $35 per month for property, Kylie?
Yeah.
So it's just an add-on, Jack.
Okay.
Where it does exactly what you said.
It makes sure that all your hours, your phone numbers, your address.
everything's synced across Google, Bing, Apple, every, like, major directory, automatically.
Patrick, can I ask you, do you, like, often see customers kind of pairing those up together?
It seems… in my mind, it makes sense to pair, like, the Google AI with the listing.
Sorry.
Yeah, we do.
work better.
Yeah.
Okay.
So, I think your tech stack is interesting because Does Opinion have that, or no?

They have a listings, they have Listings Pro, so yeah, it syndicates out to, like, the Bing, Apple Maps, everything that you just talked about, where you go in, you can update your address so it's, you know, consistent across all of those different channels.
We haven't been pushing it, and quite honestly, I want to say we maybe even have, like.
buy properties out of the 450 that are enrolled with, like, the reputation.

So, me telling you that is, like, it hasn't really been pushed or promoted, as much as the reputation piece, but yes, they do have something, but they do not have, like, the Google AI that you guys have.
But it just makes sense if we're gonna be pushing the Google AI to maybe be pushing you guys for the listings piece, too.
Versus, maybe, like, opinion.
Yeah, it's gonna be… a listings product is very similar, so it's gonna be the same function, basically.
And so, yeah, we do have some… we do have, some large third-party customers that just wrap it together with our Google product.
Okay.
It's… It's in the same world, it's a little different than reputation, so…
Yup.
Yeah, it's… I think we have $35 as an add-on.
We could add it to the Smartsheet.
I don't know if it's on there, Kylie.
We could add it as a… as a checkbox in the Smartsheet, and then…
Yes.
Okay.
Yeah.
Great, thank you.

Yeah, I can add that column in there, and that's why we put it in the Smartsheet when we… Shelby's team has seen it, and then Saad asked if we could add it in there, and our reputation, so if we're in these conversations and there's a team or two, or a portfolio that wants to add the listings, then we can just do it right there.
Okay.
That works for me.
Amazing.

Well, I might have some more questions once we really get, you know, working and onboarding with everything, but I really appreciate y'all's time this morning and letting me know about everything, and I can't wait to work more together.
Yeah.
We're looking forward to it as well.
Thank you, guys.
Thank you.
I will send over, the Google Setup Guide, so you have that, so you can see that, Reese.
I'll send over the updated one-pager, and I'll send this, deck that we just went over to.
Amazing.
Thank you, guys.
We appreciate it.
Have a good day.
Thank you.
Nice to meet you, Reece.
Bye.
Bye.
